keywords.description: if - Conditional statement. Test condition and execute commands if true.
then - Marks beginning of if statement body.
else - Alternative execution path when if condition is false.
keywords.example: if [ condition ]; then cmd1; else cmd2; fi

keywords.description: elif - Else-if for additional conditions in if statement.
keywords.example: if [ $x -eq 0 ]; then echo "zero"; elif [ $x -gt 0 ]; then echo "positive"; fi

keywords.description: fi - Closes if statement block.
for - Loop over list of items or range of numbers.
keywords.example: for i in 1 2 3; do echo $i; done; for f in *.txt; do cat $f; done

keywords.description: while - Loop while condition is true.
until - Loop until condition becomes true.
do - Marks beginning of loop body in for/while/until.
keywords.example: for i in *; do echo $i; done

keywords.description: done - Closes for/while/until loop block.
case - Multi-way conditional branching. Pattern matching statement.
keywords.example: case $var in pat1) cmd1;; pat2) cmd2;; esac

keywords.description: esac - Closes case statement block.
function - Declares a function (alternative syntax).
return - Exit function with return code (0-255).
break - Exit from for/while/until loop.
keywords.example: while true; do if [ condition ]; then break; fi; done

keywords.description: continue - Skip to next iteration of loop.
keywords.example: for i in *; do [ -d "$i" ] && continue; echo $i; done

keywords.description: exit - Terminate script with exit code (0=success, non-zero=error).
echo - Print text to standard output. Use -n to omit newline, -e for escape sequences.
read - Read input from stdin into variables. Use -p for prompt, -r for raw input.
keywords.example: read name; read -p "Enter value: " value; read -r line

keywords.description: declare - Declare variables with attributes. -a for array, -r for readonly, -i for integer.
keywords.example: declare -i count=0; declare -r CONST=10; declare -a array

keywords.description: local - Declare local variables in functions.
export - Make variables available to child processes.
readonly - Mark variables as immutable.
unset - Remove variables or functions.
shift - Shift positional parameters left. $2 becomes $1, etc.
source - Execute commands from file in current shell. Alias for '.'
cd - Change directory. Use - to go to previous directory, ~ for home.
pwd - Print working directory. -P shows physical path.
test - Evaluate conditional expressions. [ ] is alias for test.
eval - Evaluate arguments as shell command.
exec - Replace shell with command, or redirect file descriptors.
trap - Execute command on signal or script exit. Used for cleanup.
set - Set shell options. -e exits on error, -u errors on undefined vars, -x prints commands.
shopt - Set shell options. -s enables, -u disables.
getopts - Parse command-line options in scripts.
keywords.example: while getopts "ab:c" opt; do case $opt in a) ;; b) val=$OPTARG;; esac; done

keywords.description: printf - Formatted output. More portable and powerful than echo.
let - Arithmetic evaluation. Alternative to (( )).
grep - Search text using patterns. -i ignore case, -r recursive, -v invert match.
sed - Stream editor for text transformation. s/find/replace/ for substitution.
keywords.example: sed 's/old/new/' file; sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' file; sed -n '5,10p' file

keywords.description: awk - Pattern scanning and text processing. $1, $2 are fields, $0 is entire line.
find - Search for files and directories. -name for name, -type f/d for file/dir.
keywords.example: find . -name "*.txt"; find /path -type f -mtime -7; find . -exec grep pattern {} \;

keywords.description: xargs - Build and execute commands from stdin. Often used with find.
keywords.example: find . -name "*.txt" | xargs grep "pattern"; ls | xargs -I {} echo {}

keywords.description: cut - Extract sections from lines. -d for delimiter, -f for fields.
sort - Sort lines of text. -n numeric, -r reverse, -u unique.
uniq - Report or omit repeated lines. Must be sorted first. -c for counts.
keywords.example: sort file | uniq; sort file | uniq -c; sort file | uniq -d

keywords.description: tr - Translate or delete characters. Works on stdin only.
wc - Count lines, words, and bytes. -l lines, -w words, -c bytes.
keywords.example: wc -l file.txt; wc -w file.txt; ls | wc -l

keywords.description: head - Output first part of files. -n for number of lines.
keywords.example: head file.txt; head -n 20 file.txt; head -n -10 file.txt

keywords.description: tail - Output last part of files. -f follows file as it grows, -n for lines.
tee - Read from stdin and write to stdout and files. -a to append.
keywords.example: echo "text" | tee file.txt; command | tee -a log.txt

keywords.description: cat - Concatenate and display files. -n numbers lines.
keywords.example: cat file.txt; cat file1 file2 > combined; cat -n file.txt

keywords.description: ls - List directory contents. -l long format, -a all files, -h human-readable.
chmod - Change file permissions. Use octal (755) or symbolic (u+x) notation.
keywords.example: chmod 755 script.sh; chmod u+x file; chmod -R 644 *.txt

keywords.description: chown - Change file owner and group. -R for recursive.
keywords.example: chown user file; chown user:group file; chown -R user:group dir/

keywords.description: mkdir - Create directories. -p creates parent directories.
rm - Remove files or directories. -r recursive, -f force, -i interactive.
mv - Move or rename files. -i interactive, -n no clobber.
keywords.example: mv old.txt new.txt; mv file.txt dir/; mv -i source dest

keywords.description: cp - Copy files or directories. -r recursive, -p preserve attributes.
keywords.example: cp file.txt copy.txt; cp -r dir/ newdir/; cp -p file dest

keywords.description: in - Used with for and case to specify the list or patterns to iterate/match.
keywords.example: for i in 1 2 3; do echo $i; done

keywords.description: select - Creates a numbered menu from a word list and prompts the user to choose.
keywords.example: select opt in "Start" "Stop" "Quit"; do echo $opt; break; done

keywords.description: time - Reports the real, user, and system time consumed by a pipeline.
coproc - Runs a command in a coprocess with two-way pipe communication.
keywords.example: coproc myproc { while read line; do echo "got: $line"; done; }

keywords.description: alias - Define or display shell aliases. Creates shorthand for longer commands.
unalias - Remove previously defined aliases.
type - Displays how a command name would be interpreted (alias, builtin, file, function, keyword).
keywords.example: type ls  # ls is aliased to 'ls --color=auto'

keywords.description: hash - Manage the hash table of remembered command paths for faster lookups.
command - Run a command bypassing shell functions. Useful to call the real binary when a function shadows it.
builtin - Execute a shell builtin directly, bypassing functions with the same name.
enable - Enable or disable shell builtins.
keywords.example: enable -n echo  # disable the echo builtin

keywords.description: wait - Wait for background processes to finish and return their exit status.
keywords.example: cmd1 & cmd2 & wait  # wait for both

keywords.description: jobs - List active jobs started in the current shell session.
bg - Resume a suspended job in the background.
fg - Bring a background job to the foreground.
disown - Remove a job from the shell's job table so it won't receive SIGHUP on shell exit.
pushd - Push directory onto the stack and cd to it. Use popd to return.
keywords.example: pushd /var/log  # now in /var/log, previous dir saved

keywords.description: popd - Pop the top directory from the stack and cd to it.
keywords.example: popd  # return to previously pushed directory

keywords.description: dirs - Display the directory stack managed by pushd/popd.
ulimit - Get or set user resource limits (file size, open files, stack size, etc.).
umask - Get or set the file creation permission mask.
keywords.example: umask 022  # default: owner rw, group/other r-only

keywords.description: complete - Specify how arguments to a command are completed with Tab.
compgen - Generate possible completions matching a word. Used in completion functions.
compopt - Modify completion options for the current completion.
keywords.example: compopt -o nospace  # don't add space after completion

keywords.description: mapfile - Read lines from stdin into an indexed array (also called readarray).
readarray - Synonym for mapfile. Reads lines from stdin into an array.
caller - Returns the context of the current subroutine call (line number, function, file). Used for debugging.
suspend - Suspend execution of the current shell (like Ctrl+Z on itself).
basename - Strip directory and optionally suffix from a filename.
dirname - Strip the last component from a file path, returning the directory.
realpath - Resolve symlinks and relative paths to get the canonical absolute path.
mktemp - Create a temporary file or directory safely with a unique name.
date - Display or set the system date and time.
sleep - Pause execution for a specified duration.
kill - Send a signal to a process. Defaults to SIGTERM.
diff - Compare files line by line and show differences.
patch - Apply a diff/patch file to a source file.
tar - Archive and compress files.
keywords.example: tar -czf archive.tar.gz dir/  # create gzip archive

keywords.description: gzip - Compress files using the gzip algorithm.
curl - Transfer data from or to a server using URLs. Supports HTTP, FTP, and more.
wget - Non-interactive network downloader. Good for recursive downloads.
ssh - Secure shell client for remote login and command execution.
scp - Securely copy files between hosts over SSH.
rsync - Fast, versatile file copying supporting incremental transfers.
touch - Create an empty file or update the timestamp of an existing file.
ln - Create hard or symbolic links between files.
du - Estimate file and directory space usage.
df - Report filesystem disk space usage.
ps - Report a snapshot of current processes.
top - Interactive process viewer showing system resource usage.
keywords.example: top -bn1 | head -20  # batch mode, one iteration

keywords.description: env - Run a command with a modified environment or print environment variables.
which - Locate a command in the user's PATH.
whoami - Print the current effective user name.
id - Print real and effective user and group IDs.
keywords.example: id -u  # print numeric user ID only

keywords.description: su - Switch user identity. Run a shell or command as another user.
sudo - Execute a command as another user (default: root) with elevated privileges.
chgrp - Change the group ownership of files.
stat - Display detailed file or filesystem status information.
file - Determine the type of a file by examining its content.
keywords.example: file image.png  # PNG image data, 800 x 600

keywords.description: md5sum - Compute and check MD5 message digest checksums.
sha256sum - Compute and check SHA-256 checksums.
yes - Repeatedly output a line of text (default 'y'). Useful for piping into interactive commands.
seq - Print a sequence of numbers with configurable start, step, and end.
bc - Arbitrary precision calculator language.
column - Format text into aligned columns.
paste - Merge lines of files side by side.
comm - Compare two sorted files line by line, showing lines unique to each and common lines.
keywords.example: comm -23 file1.txt file2.txt  # lines only in file1

keywords.description: rev - Reverse each line character by character.
nl - Number lines of files.
fold - Wrap lines to a specified width.
keywords.example: fold -w 80 -s longfile.txt  # wrap at 80 chars, break at spaces

keywords.description: fmt - Simple text formatter that reflowed paragraphs to a given width.
nohup - Run a command immune to hangup signals, persisting after terminal close.
timeout - Run a command with a time limit, killing it if it takes too long.
watch - Execute a command periodically, showing output fullscreen.
jq - Lightweight JSON processor for querying, filtering, and transforming JSON data.
tput - Query and set terminal capabilities (colors, cursor positioning, etc.).
keywords.example: tput setaf 1  # set text color to red

keywords.description: $? - Exit status of the last executed command. 0 means success, non-zero means failure.
$# - The number of positional parameters passed to the script or function.
keywords.example: if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then echo 'Usage: script arg1 arg2'; fi

keywords.description: $@ - All positional parameters as separate words (preserves quoting per argument).
keywords.example: for arg in "$@"; do process "$arg"; done

keywords.description: $* - All positional parameters as a single word, joined by IFS.
$$ - Process ID of the current shell.
$! - Process ID of the last background command.
$0 - Name of the current script or shell.
$_ - Last argument of the previous command.
keywords.example: mkdir /tmp/test && cd $_  # cd into the just-created dir

keywords.description: BASH_VERSION - String describing the version of this Bash instance.
RANDOM - Generates a random integer between 0 and 32767 on each reference.
SECONDS - Number of seconds since the shell was started. Can be reset.
LINENO - Current line number in the script. Useful in error messages.
FUNCNAME - Array of function names in the call stack. FUNCNAME[0] is the current function.
BASH_SOURCE - Array of source filenames in the call stack. Used to find the script's own path.
PIPESTATUS - Array of exit statuses from the most recent pipeline.
IFS - Internal Field Separator. Controls word splitting for unquoted expansions (default: space/tab/newline).
PATH - Colon-separated list of directories searched for commands.
HOME - Home directory of the current user.
PWD - Current working directory (set by cd).
OLDPWD - Previous working directory (set by cd). Used by 'cd -'.

snippets.description: Bash script header with shebang
If-then-else statement
If-elif-else chain
For loop over items
For loop with range
For loop over files
snippets.content:
for ${1:file} in ${2:*.txt}; do
	[ -f "$${1:file}" ] || continue
	${3:# process file}
done

snippets.description: While loop
While read loop for processing lines
Case statement for pattern matching
Function definition
Function with argument handling
Parse command-line options
Error handling function
Check if command exists
snippets.content:
if ! command -v ${1:command} &> /dev/null; then
	echo "${1:command} not found" >&2
	exit 1
fi

snippets.description: Check if file exists
snippets.content:
if [ ! -f "${1:file.txt}" ]; then
	echo "File not found: ${1:file.txt}" >&2
	exit 1
fi

snippets.description: Check if directory exists
snippets.content:
if [ ! -d "${1:directory}" ]; then
	echo "Directory not found: ${1:directory}" >&2
	exit 1
fi

snippets.description: Trap for cleanup on exit
Read user input with prompt
snippets.content:
read -p "${1:Enter value}: " ${2:variable}
if [ -z "$${2:variable}" ]; then
	echo "No input provided" >&2
	exit 1
fi

snippets.description: Declare and use array
snippets.content:
declare -a ${1:array}=(${2:"item1" "item2" "item3"})
for ${3:item} in "${${1:array}[@]}"; do
	${4:# process item}
done

snippets.description: Declare associative array (hash map)
String comparison tests
snippets.content:
if [ -z "${1:$var}" ]; then echo "empty"; fi
if [ -n "${1:$var}" ]; then echo "not empty"; fi
if [ "${1:$var1}" = "${2:$var2}" ]; then echo "equal"; fi

snippets.description: Numeric comparison tests
snippets.content:
if [ ${1:$num1} -eq ${2:$num2} ]; then echo "equal"; fi
if [ ${1:$num1} -lt ${2:$num2} ]; then echo "less than"; fi
if [ ${1:$num1} -gt ${2:$num2} ]; then echo "greater than"; fi

snippets.description: File test conditions
snippets.content:
[ -f "${1:file}" ]  # file exists and is regular file
[ -d "${1:dir}" ]   # directory exists
[ -r "${1:file}" ]  # file is readable
[ -w "${1:file}" ]  # file is writable
[ -x "${1:file}" ]  # file is executable
[ -s "${1:file}" ]  # file exists and is not empty

snippets.description: Input/output redirection
snippets.content:
command > ${1:output.txt}          # redirect stdout
command 2> ${2:error.txt}         # redirect stderr
command &> ${3:all.txt}           # redirect both
command >> ${1:output.txt}        # append stdout
command < ${4:input.txt}          # redirect stdin

snippets.description: Pipeline with error handling
snippets.content:
${1:command1} | ${2:command2} | ${3:command3}
if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]; then
	echo "${1:command1} failed" >&2
fi

snippets.description: Here document
snippets.content:
cat <<EOF
${1:Multi-line
text content
here}
EOF

snippets.description: Here document with variable expansion
Here document without variable expansion
Command substitution
snippets.content:
${1:result}=$(${2:command})
echo "Result: $${1:result}"

snippets.description: Arithmetic expansion
snippets.content:
${1:result}=$((${2:expression}))
let ${1:result}="${2:expression}"
((${1:result} = ${2:expression}))

snippets.description: Strict mode header — fail on errors, unset vars, and pipe failures
Strict mode with debug tracing (set -x)
Get the directory where the script itself is located (resolves symlinks)
snippets.content: SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

snippets.description: Print usage/help message and exit
Parse long and short options with while/case
snippets.content:
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        -h|--help)
            usage
            ;;
        -v|--verbose)
            VERBOSE=true
            shift
            ;;
        -o|--output)
            OUTPUT="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        --)
            shift
            break
            ;;
        -*)
            echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
        *)
            ARGS+=("$1")
            shift
            ;;
    esac
done

snippets.description: Validate minimum number of arguments
snippets.content:
if [[ $# -lt ${1:2} ]]; then
    echo "Error: expected at least ${1:2} arguments, got $#" >&2
    usage
fi

snippets.description: Ensure a command is available or exit with an error
snippets.content:
for cmd in ${1:curl jq}; do
    if ! command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "Error: required command '$cmd' not found" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

snippets.description: Interactive menu with select
Retry a command up to N times with delay
snippets.content:
retry() {
    local max_attempts=${1:3}
    local delay=${2:5}
    local attempt=1
    while [[ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]]; do
        if "$@"; then
            return 0
        fi
        echo "Attempt $attempt/$max_attempts failed. Retrying in ${delay}s..." >&2
        sleep "$delay"
        ((attempt++))
    done
    echo "All $max_attempts attempts failed" >&2
    return 1
}

snippets.description: Until loop — repeats until condition becomes true
C-style for loop with arithmetic
Infinite while loop with break condition
snippets.content:
while true; do
    ${1:# body}
    if ${2:condition}; then
        break
    fi
    sleep ${3:1}
done

snippets.description: Convert string to uppercase
Convert string to lowercase
Replace pattern in a string variable

snippets.description: Trim leading/trailing whitespace from a variable
Split string into array by delimiter

snippets.description: Check if a string contains a substring
Check if a string starts with a prefix
Get the length of a string
Extract a substring by position and length

snippets.description: Regex match with Bash's =~ operator and BASH_REMATCH

snippets.description: Iterate over all elements of an array
Get the number of elements in an array
Append an element to an array
snippets.content: ${1:arr}+=("${2:new_element}")

snippets.description: Get a slice of an array
Check if an array contains a value
snippets.content:
contains() {
    local needle="$1"; shift
    for item in "$@"; do
        [[ "$item" == "$needle" ]] && return 0
    done
    return 1
}
if contains "${1:value}" "${${2:arr}[@]}"; then
    echo "found"
fi

snippets.description: Join array elements with a delimiter
Create a temporary file with automatic cleanup on exit
Create a temporary directory with automatic cleanup on exit
Read a CSV file line by line, splitting fields
snippets.content:
while IFS=',' read -r ${1:col1} ${2:col2} ${3:col3}; do
    echo "$col1 $col2 $col3"
done < "${4:file.csv}"

snippets.description: Find files and execute a command on each
snippets.content: find ${1:.} -type f -name '${2:*.log}' -exec ${3:echo} {} \;

snippets.description: Safely process find results with a while loop (handles spaces in filenames)
Create a lock file to prevent concurrent execution
snippets.content:
LOCKFILE="/tmp/$(basename "$0").lock"
if ! mkdir "$LOCKFILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Another instance is already running" >&2
    exit 1
fi
trap 'rmdir "$LOCKFILE"' EXIT

snippets.description: Logging function with timestamp and severity levels
Simple progress bar for loops
snippets.content:
total=${1:100}
for ((i=1; i<=total; i++)); do
    pct=$((i * 100 / total))
    printf '\r[%-50s] %d%%' "$(printf '#%.0s' $(seq 1 $((pct/2))))" "$pct"
done
echo

snippets.description: Print error message and exit with failure
snippets.content: die() { echo "Error: $*" >&2; exit 1; }

snippets.description: Trap multiple signals for proper cleanup
Trap ERR to print a stack trace on failure (with set -e)
snippets.content: trap 'echo "Error at ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}:${LINENO} in ${FUNCNAME[0]:-main}" >&2' ERR

snippets.description: Try-catch pattern using subshell and error capture
snippets.content:
if output=$(${1:risky_command} 2>&1); then
    echo "Success: $output"
else
    rc=$?
    echo "Failed (exit $rc): $output" >&2
fi

snippets.description: Run commands in parallel with a job limit
snippets.content:
MAX_JOBS=${1:4}
for ${2:item} in ${3:"${items[@]}"}; do
    (
        ${4:process "$item"}
    ) &
    # Limit parallel jobs
    while [[ $(jobs -rp | wc -l) -ge $MAX_JOBS ]]; do
        wait -n
    done
done
wait  # wait for all remaining jobs

snippets.description: Run tasks in background and wait for all to finish
snippets.content:
pids=()
${1:command1} & pids+=($!)
${2:command2} & pids+=($!)

for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    wait "$pid" || echo "Process $pid failed" >&2
done

snippets.description: HTTP GET request with JSON parsing
snippets.content:
response=$(curl -sS -H 'Content-Type: application/json' "${1:https://api.example.com/endpoint}")
echo "$response" | jq '${2:.}'

snippets.description: HTTP POST request with JSON body
snippets.content:
curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{${1:"key": "value"}}' \
    "${2:https://api.example.com/endpoint}"

snippets.description: Check if a port is open on a host
snippets.content:
if timeout 3 bash -c "</dev/tcp/${1:localhost}/${2:8080}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Port ${2:8080} is open"
else
    echo "Port ${2:8080} is closed"
fi

snippets.description: Read a key=value config file, ignoring comments and blank lines
snippets.content:
while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
    [[ -z "$key" || "$key" == \#* ]] && continue
    export "$key=$value"
done < "${1:config.env}"

snippets.description: Set a variable to a default if not already set
snippets.content: ${1:VAR}="${${1:VAR}:-${2:default_value}}"

snippets.description: Ensure a variable is set or exit with error
snippets.content: : "${${1:VAR}:?Error: ${1:VAR} is not set}"

snippets.description: Generate an ISO-format timestamp
snippets.content: timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z')

snippets.description: Measure elapsed time of a block of code
snippets.content:
start_time=$SECONDS
${1:# code to time}
elapsed=$((SECONDS - start_time))
echo "Elapsed: ${elapsed}s"

snippets.description: Compare two command outputs using process substitution
snippets.content: diff <(${1:command1}) <(${2:command2})

snippets.description: Redirect both stdout and stderr to a file
snippets.content: ${1:command} > "${2:output.log}" 2>&1

snippets.description: Log output to file while still showing on screen
snippets.content: ${1:command} 2>&1 | tee "${2:output.log}"

snippets.description: Open a custom file descriptor for writing
snippets.content:
exec 3> "${1:logfile.txt}"
echo "logged to fd 3" >&3
exec 3>&-  # close fd 3

items.name: set
items.description: Set or unset shell options and positional parameters (-e, -u, -o pipefail, etc.)

items.name: shopt
items.description: Set or unset optional shell behaviors (extglob, globstar, nullglob, etc.)

items.name: trap
items.description: Register commands to execute when signals or events occur (EXIT, INT, ERR, etc.)

items.name: declare
items.description: Declare variables with attributes: -a (array), -A (assoc), -i (integer), -r (readonly), -x (export)

items.name: local
items.description: Declare a function-local variable, preventing outer scope pollution

items.name: export
items.description: Mark variables for export to child processes

items.name: readonly
items.description: Mark variables as read-only, preventing modification or unsetting

items.name: eval
items.description: Evaluate arguments as a shell command. Use sparingly due to security risks.

items.name: exec
items.description: Replace the shell process with a command, or redirect file descriptors permanently

items.name: source
items.description: Read and execute commands from a file in the current shell (synonym: '.')

items.name: printf
items.description: Formatted output with printf-style format strings (%s, %d, %f, etc.)

items.name: read
items.description: Read a line from stdin into variables. Flags: -r (raw), -a (array), -p (prompt), -t (timeout)

items.name: test
items.description: Evaluate conditional expressions. Synonym for [ ]. Use [[ ]] for pattern matching.

items.name: getopts
items.description: Parse short command-line options inside a while loop

items.name: shift
items.description: Shift positional parameters left by N positions (default 1)

items.name: return
items.description: Return from a shell function with an optional exit status

items.name: exit
items.description: Exit the shell with an optional status code (0 = success, 1-255 = error)

items.name: echo
items.description: Display a line of text. For portable formatting, prefer printf.

items.name: unset
items.description: Unset variables or functions, removing them from the environment

items.name: wait
items.description: Wait for background jobs to finish. Returns the exit status of the last waited job.

items.name: mapfile
items.description: Read lines from stdin into an array. Alias: readarray. Flags: -t (trim newlines)

items.name: grep
items.description: Search text using patterns. Flags: -r (recursive), -i (case-insensitive), -n (line numbers), -E (extended regex)

items.name: sed
items.description: Stream editor for text transformations. Common: sed 's/old/new/g', sed -i (in-place)

items.name: awk
items.description: Pattern scanning and text processing language. Column extraction: awk '{print $1}'

items.name: find
items.description: Search directory tree for files matching criteria (-name, -type, -mtime, -exec)

items.name: sort
items.description: Sort lines. Flags: -n (numeric), -r (reverse), -k (column), -u (unique), -t (delimiter)

items.name: uniq
items.description: Filter adjacent duplicate lines. Flags: -c (count), -d (show dupes only), -u (unique only)

items.name: cut
items.description: Extract fields/columns from lines. Flags: -d (delimiter), -f (fields), -c (characters)

items.name: tr
items.description: Translate or delete characters. Examples: tr 'a-z' 'A-Z', tr -d '\n'

items.name: xargs
items.description: Build and execute commands from stdin. Flags: -I{} (replace string), -P (parallel), -0 (null-delimited)

items.name: tee
items.description: Read stdin and write to both stdout and one or more files simultaneously

items.name: wc
items.description: Count lines (-l), words (-w), characters (-c), or bytes of input

items.name: head
items.description: Output the first N lines of input (default 10). Flag: -n

items.name: tail
items.description: Output the last N lines of input. Flags: -n (lines), -f (follow/stream)

items.name: cat
items.description: Concatenate and display file contents. Flags: -n (number lines), -A (show all chars)

items.name: less
items.description: Interactive pager for viewing file contents. Supports search (/) and navigation.

items.name: diff
items.description: Compare files line by line. Flags: -u (unified), -r (recursive), --color

items.name: tar
items.description: Archive utility. Create: tar -czf archive.tar.gz dir/. Extract: tar -xzf archive.tar.gz

items.name: curl
items.description: Transfer data to/from URLs. Flags: -s (silent), -o (output), -X (method), -H (header), -d (data)

items.name: jq
items.description: JSON processor. Query: jq '.key', filter: jq '.[] | select(.x>1)', format: jq '.'

items.name: date
items.description: Display/set date-time. Format: date '+%Y-%m-%d'. Arithmetic: date -d '+1 day'

items.name: mktemp
items.description: Create unique temporary files (-d for directories). Pattern: mktemp /tmp/myapp.XXXXXX

items.name: grep
items.description: Search patterns in files. Extended (-E), Perl (-P), recursive (-r), invert (-v)

items.name: sed
items.description: Stream editor. Substitute: s/old/new/g. Delete: /pattern/d. In-place: -i

items.name: awk
items.description: Text processing. Fields: $1,$2. Math: awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}'. Printf support.

items.name: cut
items.description: Extract columns/fields. By delimiter: cut -d',' -f1,3. By chars: cut -c1-10

items.name: tr
items.description: Character translation/deletion. Uppercase: tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'. Squeeze: tr -s ' '

items.name: sort
items.description: Sort lines. Numeric: -n. Reverse: -r. Column: -k2. Unique: -u. Stable: -s

items.name: uniq
items.description: Remove adjacent duplicates. Count: -c. Dupes only: -d. Case-insensitive: -i

items.name: paste
items.description: Merge lines side by side. Delimiter: -d','

items.name: column
items.description: Align text. Table mode: -t. Separator: -s','

items.name: nl
items.description: Number lines. All lines: -ba

items.name: rev
items.description: Reverse characters on each line

items.name: fold
items.description: Wrap long lines. Width: -w80. Break at words: -s

items.name: fmt
items.description: Reflow text to width. Default 75 chars.

items.name: comm
items.description: Compare sorted files. Unique to file1: -23. Common: -12

items.name: wc
items.description: Count lines (-l), words (-w), bytes (-c) in input

items.name: cp
items.description: Copy files/dirs. Recursive: -r. Preserve: -p. Interactive: -i. Archive: -a

items.name: mv
items.description: Move/rename files. Interactive: -i. No overwrite: -n. Verbose: -v

items.name: rm
items.description: Remove files. Recursive: -r. Force: -f. Interactive: -i

items.name: mkdir
items.description: Create directories. Parents: -p. Mode: -m 0755

items.name: rmdir
items.description: Remove empty directories. Parents: -p

items.name: touch
items.description: Create empty files or update timestamps

items.name: ln
items.description: Create links. Symbolic: -s. Force: -f

items.name: chmod
items.description: Change permissions. Recursive: -R. Octal: 755. Symbolic: u+x

items.name: chown
items.description: Change file owner/group. Recursive: -R. Format: user:group

items.name: stat
items.description: File status. Format: -c '%s %Y' (size, mtime)

items.name: file
items.description: Detect file type by content inspection

items.name: basename
items.description: Strip directory prefix. Optional suffix removal: basename f.txt .txt

items.name: dirname
items.description: Strip last path component, returning the directory portion

items.name: realpath
items.description: Resolve to canonical absolute path, following symlinks

items.name: find
items.description: Search directory tree. Name: -name. Type: -type f. Time: -mtime. Action: -exec

items.name: du
items.description: Disk usage. Summary: -s. Human: -h. Depth: --max-depth=1

items.name: df
items.description: Filesystem space. Human: -h. Type: -T. Inode: -i

items.name: ps
items.description: Process status. Full listing: ps aux. Tree: ps axjf. By PID: ps -p PID

items.name: kill
items.description: Send signal to process. SIGTERM: kill PID. SIGKILL: kill -9 PID. List: kill -l

items.name: killall
items.description: Kill processes by name. Signal: killall -9 name

items.name: pkill
items.description: Kill processes matching a pattern. Full cmdline: -f

items.name: pgrep
items.description: Find PIDs matching a pattern. List names: -l. Full: -f

items.name: top
items.description: Interactive process monitor. Batch: -bn1. Sort by mem: Shift+M

items.name: nohup
items.description: Run command immune to hangup signals, persisting after terminal close

items.name: timeout
items.description: Run with time limit. Signal: --signal=KILL. Foreground: --foreground

items.name: nice
items.description: Run with modified scheduling priority. Lower: nice -n 10 cmd

items.name: wait
items.description: Wait for background process. By PID: wait $PID. Any: wait -n

items.name: jobs
items.description: List background jobs. With PIDs: -l. Running: -r

items.name: bg
items.description: Resume suspended job in background

items.name: fg
items.description: Bring background job to foreground

items.name: curl
items.description: HTTP client. GET: curl URL. POST: -X POST -d data. Headers: -H. Follow redirects: -L

items.name: wget
items.description: Download files. Quiet: -q. Output: -O file. Recursive: -r. Continue: -c

items.name: ssh
items.description: Remote login. Port: -p. Key: -i. Tunnel: -L local:host:remote

items.name: scp
items.description: Secure copy. Port: -P. Recursive: -r. Format: user@host:path

items.name: rsync
items.description: Efficient sync. Archive: -a. Verbose: -v. Compress: -z. Dry-run: -n. Delete: --delete

items.name: nc
items.description: Netcat: TCP/UDP connections. Listen: -l. Port scan: -z. UDP: -u

items.name: ss
items.description: Socket statistics. Listening: -l. TCP: -t. UDP: -u. Processes: -p. Numeric: -n

items.name: ip
items.description: Network config. Show: ip addr. Routes: ip route. Links: ip link

items.name: dig
items.description: DNS lookup. Short: +short. Trace: +trace. Type: -t MX

items.name: host
items.description: Simple DNS lookup. Reverse: host IP

items.name: ping
items.description: Test connectivity. Count: -c 3. Timeout: -W 2

items.name: ${var:-default}
items.description: Use default value if var is unset or empty

items.name: ${var:=default}
items.description: Assign default if var is unset or empty, then expand

items.name: ${var:+alternate}
items.description: Use alternate value if var IS set and non-empty

items.name: ${var:?message}
items.description: Error with message if var is unset or empty (exits in non-interactive shell)

items.name: ${#var}
items.description: String length of var

items.name: ${var#pattern}
items.description: Remove shortest prefix matching pattern

items.name: ${var##pattern}
items.description: Remove longest prefix matching pattern

items.name: ${var%pattern}
items.description: Remove shortest suffix matching pattern

items.name: ${var%%pattern}
items.description: Remove longest suffix matching pattern

items.name: ${var/old/new}
items.description: Replace first match of old with new

items.name: ${var//old/new}
items.description: Replace all matches of old with new

items.name: ${var^}
items.description: Uppercase first character

items.name: ${var^^}
items.description: Uppercase all characters

items.name: ${var,}
items.description: Lowercase first character

items.name: ${var,,}
items.description: Lowercase all characters

items.name: ${var:offset:length}
items.description: Substring extraction starting at offset for length characters

items.name: ${!prefix@}
items.description: All variable names matching prefix

items.name: ${!arr[@]}
items.description: All indices/keys of an array

items.name: -e
items.description: True if file exists (any type)

items.name: -f
items.description: True if file exists and is a regular file

items.name: -d
items.description: True if file exists and is a directory

items.name: -L
items.description: True if file exists and is a symbolic link

items.name: -s
items.description: True if file exists and has size greater than zero

items.name: -r
items.description: True if file exists and is readable

items.name: -w
items.description: True if file exists and is writable

items.name: -x
items.description: True if file exists and is executable

items.name: -z
items.description: True if string is empty (zero length)

items.name: -n
items.description: True if string is non-empty (non-zero length)

items.name: -eq
items.description: Integer equal

items.name: -ne
items.description: Integer not equal

items.name: -lt
items.description: Integer less than

items.name: -le
items.description: Integer less than or equal

items.name: -gt
items.description: Integer greater than

items.name: -ge
items.description: Integer greater than or equal
String equal (in [[ ]], also supports glob patterns)
String not equal
Regex match (only in [[ ]]). Captures in BASH_REMATCH array.

items.name: -nt
items.description: True if file1 is newer than file2 (by modification time)

items.name: -ot
items.description: True if file1 is older than file2

items.name: -a
items.description: Logical AND inside [ ]. In [[ ]], use && instead.

items.name: -o
items.description: Logical OR inside [ ]. In [[ ]], use || instead.
